page: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is an entry-level virtual server based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing a QEMU hypervisor. It features a single shared AMD EPYC virtual CPU core running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes 40 GB of local SSD storage, while no dedicated GPU hardware is present. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16 variants) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core capabilities and overall system throughput. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, ranking in the top 25%. However, due to the single-core shared allocation, multi-core performance and Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with small-block read-only bandwidth performing in the bottom 10%, though large-block write bandwidth achieves an average rating.

Real-world workload simulations reflect these hardware limitations. Static web serving throughput and database operations score in the bottom 10%, and Redis SET operations reach 2.79 million operations per second. Conversely, single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at an average level. Small LLM inference text generation also reaches an average speed of 107.47 tokens per second, though prompt processing remains weak.

Given its shared CPU architecture and modest memory footprint, the cpx12 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic utility tasks, lightweight single-threaded applications, development environments, or simple web serving where resource demands are minimal and predictable.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is an entry-level virtual server featuring a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 40 GB of local SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture under a QEMU hypervisor, this instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AES cryptography. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests. However, memory bandwidth, multi-core scaling, and database operations reside in the lower percentiles due to the single-core shared resource model. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and small LLM text generation perform at average levels. The cpx12 represents a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads, making it suitable for basic development environments, simple utility scripts, and low-traffic web serving.

tagline: A budget-friendly shared-core virtual server offering strong single-threaded performance and local SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads.
